Dear Sir or Madam:
RE: POTENTIAL CONFIJICT OF INTEREST
I would like to receive a written response on how to avoid a
potential conflict of interest that may arise out of my co-
ownership of a piece of real property in the City limits.
I am a member of the Sacramento City Council representing District
L. My husband, Alan MoII, is a general contractor. 
He and I are
in the process of purchasing a sma11 piece of property (one-haIf
acre) on the northeast corner of Colfax and El Monte in District 
2
(see map) for the purpose of building a shop and storage area for
his business, Alan Moll Construction.
f want to be sure to avoid any potential conflicts of interest,
real or perceived, in my position on the City Council and am
seeking your advice. I would tike to receive any general
guidelines that nay be helpful and u'oul-d also iike to recei-ve your
analysis and recommendation of four specific issues.
The first issue is the possible formation of a redevelopment area
that could encompass this property. Would the formation of such an
area affect the value of the property enougth to require me to
refrain from voting or discussing this matter? If so, are there
any decisions regarding the redevelopment area, such as EIRts,
establishment of boundaries that do not affect the property, land
use p1ans, budget, etc. that I could vote on or discuss once the
redevelopment area is established?
The second issue is a major, rnulti-nillion 
dollar road improvement,
the Arden-Garden Connector, that would be located one block north
of the property. Could I vote on or di-scuss such a project?


Fair PoliticaI Practices Commission
February 14, 7-991
Page 2
The third issue is the potential hiring of a coordinator to assist
in finding retail and office users to occupy space al-ong DeL Paso
B1vd., which is l-ocated about one block south of the property. 
May
I di.scuss or vote on the hirj.ng of this coordinator?
The fourth is the establ,ishment of an artists' 
live/work space two
blocks northwest of the property. The city Council, acting as the
Redevelopment Agency, has approved the conversion of existing
warehouse space into work/1ive spaces for artists a few blocks
away. Tf other votes come up on this project, may I participate?
In a separate but related situation, I am co-ol^/ner of a duplex in
the Land Park area of District 4. Are there special situations
related to rentaL property that I should be aware of to avoid
conflicts related to this property as wel,I?
Thank you for your assistance and guidance in these matters.
S incerely,

sections.question: I would like to receive a written response on how to avoid a potential conflict of interest that may arise out of my co-ownership of a piece of real property in the City limits. I am a member of the Sacramento City Council representing District 1. My husband, Alan Moll, is a general contractor. He and I are in the process of purchasing a small piece of property (one-half acre) on the northeast corner of Colfax and El Monte in District 2 (see map) for the purpose of building a shop and storage area for his business, Alan Moll Construction. I want to be sure to avoid any potential conflicts of interest, real or perceived, in my position on the City Council and am seeking your advice. I would like to receive any general guidelines that may be helpful and would also like to receive your analysis and recommendation of four specific issues. [Four specific issues detailed regarding: (1) potential redevelopment area formation affecting property value; (2) Arden-Garden Connector road improvement one block north; (3) hiring of coordinator for Del Paso Blvd retail/office users; (4) artists' live/work space project two blocks northwest.] In a separate but related situation, I am co-owner of a duplex in the Land Park area of District 4. Are there special situations related to rental property that I should be aware of to avoid conflicts related to this property as well?

sections.question_synthetic: City Councilmember Heather Fargo seeks advice on avoiding conflicts of interest related to her co-ownership of real property in the city limits where her husband operates a construction business, and requests analysis of four specific development/improvement projects near the property and guidance on her rental property holdings.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request, not an FPPC response letter. No CONCLUSION or advice section present. Document is dated February 14, 1991 and addressed to FPPC. OCR quality is moderate with some formatting irregularities but content is readable.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: I would like to receive a written response on how to avoid a potential conflict of interest that may arise out of my co-ownership of a piece of real property in the City limits. I am a member of the Sacramento City Council representing District 1. My husband, Alan Moll, is a general contractor. He and I are in the process of purchasing a small piece of property (one-half acre) on the northeast corner of Colfax and El Monte in District 2 (see map) for the purpose of building a shop and storage area for his business, Alan Moll Construction. I want to be sure to avoid any potential conflicts of interest, real or perceived, in my position on the City Council and am seeking your advice. I would like to receive any general guidelines that may be helpful and would also like to receive your analysis and recommendation of four specific issues. [Four specific issues detailed regarding: (1) potential redevelopment area formation affecting property value; (2) Arden-Garden Connector road improvement one block north; (3) hiring of coordinator for Del Paso Blvd retail/office users; (4) artists' live/work space project two blocks northwest.] In a separate but related situation, I am co-owner of a duplex in the Land Park area of District 4. Are there special situations related to rental property that I should be aware of to avoid conflicts related to this property as well?

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from Sacramento City Councilmember Heather Fargo to the FPPC requesting written guidance on potential conflicts of interest arising from her co-ownership of real property with her contractor husband and asking for analysis of four specific projects that could affect the property's value.
